creator: stefan@FreeBSD.org
text:
RandomUser, 18.12.08, 04:07h CET:

> Stefan Walter wrote:
> > any news on this issue? Judging from your report, this is a problem either
> > in pdcurses or in the editor, which would mean it should be fixed with the
> > upstream authors, not in the FreeBSD ports collection. Or can you verify
> > that the problem only exists on FreeBSD systems?
> 
> So far, I know that the THE-3.1 + PDCurses-3.3 issue only exists with
> FreeBSD. The only other systems that I have are Solaris-10 Sparc and
> Solaris-10 X86. I have a partial build on Solaris Sparc but nothing on
> Solaris X86. I have copied the source archives for THE and PDCurses to
> Solaris X86 and will see what will work under both flavors of Solaris-10.
> 
> Every version of FreeBSD that I have tried with THE-3.1 and PDCurses-3.3
> has failed. I vaguely remember something about the changes reported in
> PDCurses-3.3 (pointers, data types, headers, ?) that implied a possible
> problem for programs working with PDCurses-3.2.

OK, if the development of pdcurses brought such incompatibilities, the
current version of editors/the might plainly be too old. The port doesn't
have a maintainer - have you tried updating it to a more recent version
(there is a 3.3beta at sourceforge.net)?

Regards,
Stefan
